On this page

Action Hooks ​

Action hooks allow you to execute custom code at specific points in the plugin's lifecycle. Use add_action() to hook into these.

air_services_loaded ​

Fires after core services (Encryption, Template Engine, Groq) are initialized, but before components (Settings Page, Image Uploader) are set up.

Use this to modify or replace services before they are used by other components.

php
add_action( 'air_services_loaded', function ( \AIR\Plugin $plugin ): void {
    // Access services early
    $groq = $plugin->get_groq_service();
    $encryption = $plugin->get_encryption_service();
} );
ParameterTypeDescription
$plugin\AIR\PluginThe plugin instance

Source: Plugin::init()

air_loaded ​

Fires after the plugin is fully initialized — all services and components are ready.

This is the recommended hook for Pro add-ons to register their features.

php
add_action( 'air_loaded', function ( \AIR\Plugin $plugin ): void {
    // Plugin is fully loaded, safe to add features
    if ( $plugin->is_pro_active() ) {
        // Initialize Pro features
    }
} );
ParameterTypeDescription
$plugin\AIR\PluginThe plugin instance

Source: Plugin::init()

air_api_response ​

Fires after a response is received from the Groq Vision API. Useful for logging, analytics, or debugging.

php
add_action( 'air_api_response', function ( ?array $decoded, int $code, string $image_path ): void {
    if ( 200 === $code ) {
        error_log( 'AIR: Successful API response for ' . basename( $image_path ) );
    } else {
        error_log( 'AIR: API returned HTTP ' . $code );
    }
}, 10, 3 );
ParameterTypeDescription
$decodedarray|nullThe decoded JSON response body
$codeintThe HTTP response code
$image_pathstringPath to the image file

Source: Groq_Service::generate_description()

air_image_renamed ​

Fires after an image has been successfully renamed. Use this for logging, tracking rename history, or triggering follow-up actions.

php
add_action( 'air_image_renamed', function ( string $new_filename, string $original_name, string $description, array $file ): void {
    error_log( sprintf(
        'AIR: Renamed "%s" → "%s" (description: %s)',
        $original_name,
        $new_filename,
        $description
    ) );
}, 10, 4 );
ParameterTypeDescription
$new_filenamestringThe new filename
$original_namestringThe original filename
$descriptionstringThe AI-generated description
$filearrayThe file data array

Source: Image_Uploader::process_upload()

air_register_settings_fields ​

Fires after all core settings fields are registered. Use this to add custom settings sections and fields to the plugin's settings page.

php
add_action( 'air_register_settings_fields', function ( string $page_slug ): void {
    add_settings_section(
        'my_custom_section',
        'My Custom Settings',
        function () {
            echo '<p>Additional settings for my extension.</p>';
        },
        $page_slug
    );

    add_settings_field(
        'my_custom_field',
        'Custom Option',
        function () {
            echo '<input type="text" name="air_options[my_custom_option]" />';
        },
        $page_slug,
        'my_custom_section'
    );
} );
ParameterTypeDescription
$page_slugstringThe settings page slug (ai-image-renamer)

Source: Settings_Page::register_settings()
